author: felicepantaleo
body:
On top of https://github.com/cms-sw/cmssw/pull/45216 to check in a real-life workflow that it works. 

Migrate the double/vdouble reconstruction parameters in RecoHGCal/TICL and in the HGCAL layer-clustering algorithms (RecoLocalCalo/HGCalRecProducers, both the legacy CPU HGCalCLUEAlgo/HGCalLayerClusterProducer and the Alpaka SoA producers) to float/vfloat, and update the corresponding offline and Phase-2 HLT configurations.

Many reco parameters are cut thresholds, angular/geometric windows, working points and per-layer weights carrying at most a few significant figures, and they are compared against data that is already stored as float (layer clusters, tracksters, SoA columns). Storing them as float aligns the parameter type with the data, removes redundant double->float narrowing at the read sites, and is a first step toward propagating float precision through the reco chain.
title: move HGCAL configuration parameters from double to float
